sections.heading: 1.1 PURPOSE OF THIS DOCUMENT
sections.content: This document  describes an  ISO 8583  based  Host -to-Host  financial  card  transaction s message protocol  which is supported by Tandem Base24 POS application system.  It describes the different message types , message structures, message contents  and token s used in the ISO 8583 format. This message protocol is based on ISO 8583:1 987 version which is bitmap driven,  variable length, variable content message that can be configured differently, based on the type of message being sent.

sections.content: BASE24  Applications software (card authorisations) supplied by ACI Ltd COTS  Consumer off -the shelf device Cryptography  Cryptography is the art of Secret writing. It is art and science of using mathematics to secure information and high degree of trust in the electronic realm

sections.content: Data Key  Is an encryption Key which is created from a number of selected fields within the message (fields are agreed between the two communicating parties) and is used to encrypt / decrypt the complete message Decryption  Decryption is the reverse of encryption. It is the transformation of encrypted data back into an intelligible form

sections.heading: 0100 Authorisation request message:
sections.content: An authoris ation request (0100) message requests approval authorisation  for the transaction to proceed further. This transaction is not intended to charge or bill the cardholder account. An authoris ation request response (0110)  message  is expected in return for the 0100 message, either approving or denying the request.

sections.heading: 0110 Authorisation Response Message:
sections.content: An authorisation  request response message is returned in response to an Authorisation Request (0100) message to approve or deny the request.

sections.heading: 0200 Financial Request Message:
sections.content: A Financial Transaction Request (0200) message requests approval for a transaction that, if approved, can be immediately applied to the account of the customer for billing or statement purposes. A Financial Transaction Request Response (0210) message is expected in return  for the 0200 message, either approving or denying the request.

sections.heading: 0210 Financial Response Message:
sections.content: The Financial Transaction Request Response (0210) message is returned in  response to a Financial Transaction Request (0200) message to approve or deny  the request. Prior agreement with Barclaycard Payment Solutions must be sought prioro to using these message types.

sections.heading: HISO 8583 H2H MERCHANT  SPECIFICATION
sections.content: HISO 8583 Merchant Specification V3.9.9.docx  Page 10 of 119 Version : 3. 9.9

sections.heading: 0120 Authorisation Advice Request Message:
sections.content: The Authorisation Advice (0120) Message contains information pertaining to a previous transaction that was approved on behalf of the Card Issuer. The purpose is to inform the Card Issuer about the details of this transaction. It is not intended to permit application of the  transaction  to the cardholder account for the purpose of issuing a bill or statement.

sections.heading: 0121 Authorisation Advice Repeat Request Message:
sections.content: An Authori sation Advice Repeat (0121) message is identical to an Authorisation  Advice (0120) message, except that it denotes to the receiver that it is a possible  duplicate message. A 0121 message is used when an acknowledgment was  expected to a 0120 message but never received.

sections.heading: 0130 Authorisation Advice  Response Message:
sections.content: An Authorisation Advice Response (0130) acknowledges the receipt of an  Authorisation Advice (0120) message or an Authorisation Advice Repeat (0121)  message.

sections.heading: 0220 Financial Advice Request Message:
sections.content: A Financial Transaction Advice (0220) message advises of a previously completed  financial transaction.

sections.heading: 0221 Financial Advice Repeat Request Message:
sections.content: A Financial Transaction Advice Repeat (0221) message is identical to a Financial  Transaction Advice (0220) message, except that it denotes to the receiver that it is  a possible duplicate message. A 0221 message is used when an acknowledgment  was expected to a 0220 message but never received.

sections.heading: 0230 Financial Advice Response Message:
sections.content: A Financial Transaction Advice Response (0230) message acknowledges the receipt of a Financial Transaction Advice (0220) or Financial Transaction Advice Repeat (0221) message.

sections.heading: HISO 8583 H2H MERCHANT  SPECIFICATION
sections.content: HISO 8583 Merchant Specification V3.9.9.docx  Page 11 of 119 Version : 3. 9.9

sections.heading: 0420 Reversal Request Message:
sections.content: The reversal transaction can originate from the Terminal, Merchant Host System or from the Base24 to the Card Issuer. The purpose is to restore an account when an approved response has been received but e.g. the purchase could not be finali sed. An Acquirer Reversal Advice (0420) message reverses an earlier transaction or  authorisation .

sections.heading: 0421 Reversal Advice Repeat Request Message:
sections.content: A Reversal Advice Repeat (0421) message is identical to a Reversal Advice (0420) message, except that it denotes to the receiver that it is a  possible duplicate message. A 0421 message is used when an acknowledgment  was expected to a 0420 message but never received.

sections.heading: 0430 Reversal Advice  Response Message:
sections.content: A Reversal Advice Response (0430) message acknowledges an Acquirer Reversal  Advice (0420) message or an Acquirer Reversal Advice Repeat (0421) message.

sections.heading: 0800 Network Management Request Message:
sections.content: A Network Management Request (0800) message is used to send echo -testand logon messages.

sections.heading: 0810 Network Management Response Message:
sections.content: A Network Management Request Response (0810) message is returned in  response to a Network Management Request (0800) message.

sections.heading: 3 H2H INTERFACE
sections.content: The message format used is ASCII for all message types.

sections.heading: 3.1 MESSAGE TYPES  IDENTIFIER
sections.content: Message Type Identifier (MTI) identifies which transaction type is being handled. This field is 4 bytes and the following values are allowed:

sections.heading: 0110  Authorisation request response  Returned in response to a 0100
sections.content: Authorisation request 0120/0121  Authorisation advice / repeat advice  Fulfills  a previous authorisation request or advises of a stand -in transaction

sections.heading: 0210  Financial transaction request response  Returned in response to a 0200
sections.content: 0220/0221  Financial transaction advice / repeat advice  Fulfills  a previous authorisation request or advises of a stand -in transaction

sections.heading: 0230  Financial transaction advice response  Returned in response to a 0220/0221
sections.content: 0420/0421  Reversal advice / repeat advice  Reversal of a previous authorisation or financial transaction request.

sections.heading: 0800  Network Management request
sections.content: message  Used to perform echo tests

sections.heading: 0810  Network management response
sections.content: message  Returned in response to a 0800

sections.heading: 3.2.1  START OF MESSAGE  HEADER
sections.content: The start of message Header indicator is used to mark the start of the actual message. This field must contain the literal "ISO" .  For incoming request messages into the Barclaycard host they are always required, for outgoing response messages  from the Barclaycard host , they are always present .

sections.heading: 3.2.2  MESSAGE HEADER
sections.content: The BASE24 Header contains only mandatory fields and must have a fixed length so the message can be parsed correctly.

sections.heading: 3.3 HANDLING OF ERRONEOUS MESSAGES
sections.content: If the ISO Host Interface process receives an external message that it cannot process or reformat for internal use (because of bad data or a security  related failure), it rejects the message as follows:

sections.heading: 1. Changes the first position of the message type to 9 (for example, a 0200 message would be
sections.content: changed to a 9200 message, and a 0420 message would be changed to a 9420 message).

sections.heading: 2. Indicates the reason the message was rejected in the status field of the BASE24 external message
sections.content: header. If the message was rejected because of a security failure, the status field is set to a value of

sections.heading: 199. If the message was rejected because of bad data, this field is set to the bit number of the data
sections.content: element causing rejection (for example, if the Track 2 data in P -35 cannot be parsed, the status field in the header would be set to 035).

sections.heading: 3. Returns the message to the host.
sections.content: The above actions are taken on any message type that cannot be processed, not just those that require a response.

sections.heading: 3.4 PRIMARY BITMAP
sections.content: This field is used to identify which data components P1 to P64 that are included in the message. It is a 64 bit array (8 byte) where the bits are numbered 1 to 64, left to right. If e.g. bit 17 is '1', the data field P -17 is present in the message, if bit 17 is '0', the data field P -17 is not present in the message. Since the BASE24 external message implementation o f the ISO 8583 standard doesn't allow binary data fields, the bit map is transmitted in display format such that for each 4 -bit nibble of the bit map, the display character code of the corresponding hexadecimal digit is transmitted. This means that the 8 b yte binary bit map will be extended to 16 byte, each containing the ASCII character code representing the display characters 0 -9 or A -F. Example: A possible primary bit map for an authorisation  response (purchase)

sections.heading: HISO 8583 H2H MERCHANT  SPECIFICATION
sections.content: HISO 8583 Merchant Specification V3.9.9.docx  Page 31 of 119 Version : 3. 9.9

sections.heading: 3.6.1  SECONDARY BITMAP  (P-1)
sections.content: The secondary bit map identifies the presence or absence of data elements 65 through 128 in the BASE24 external message. It functions the same as the primary bit map, except that the primary bit map identifies the presence or absence of data elements 1 through 64 and the secondary bit map identifies the presence or absence of data elements 65 through 128. The secondary bit map is required if any of data elements 65 through 128 are included in the message. Otherwise, it is not used. The presence or absence of the secondary bit map is identified by bit position 1 in the primary bit map. Data elements 65 through 128 cannot be included in the message if the secondary bit map is not present.

sections.heading: 3.6.2  PRIMARY ACCOUNT NUMBER  (P-2)
sections.content: The Primary Account Number data element contains the primary account number (PAN) of the cardholder account involved in the transaction This data element is required in case of manual or keyed transaction and mandatory for reversal request (0420) messages.

sections.heading: 3.6.3  PROCESSING CODE  (P-3)
sections.content: The Processing Code data element contains a series of digits used to describe the effect of a transaction on the customer account and the accounts affected. Valid Values: Position 1 -2 Position 3 -4 Position 5 -6 Description

sections.heading: 20 nn nn Refund (returns , payment of
sections.content: winnings )

sections.heading: 81 nn nn Account Verification
sections.content: A value of ‘00’ in position 3 -4 indicates a default From Account Type and a value of ’00’ in position 5 -

sections.heading: HISO 8583 H2H MERCHANT  SPECIFICATION
sections.content: HISO 8583 Merchant Specification V3.9.9.docx  Page 32 of 119 Version : 3. 9.9

sections.heading: 3.6.4  TRANSACTION AMOUNT  (P-4)
sections.content: The Transaction Amount data element contains the amount of funds requested (either for debit or credit) in the currency of the source location of the transaction. This data field may sometimes carry zero amount  (000000000000)  in 0120/0121, 0220/0221 messages.

sections.heading: For Diners/Discover /Visa  Account Verification and MasterCard/Maestro Account Status Inquiry
sections.content: Service transactions this field must carry a zero amount. This data field may contain an amount which differs (typically lower) to the original authori sed amount for an 0420 reversal request.

sections.heading: 3.6.5  TRANSMISSION DATE AND TIME (P-7)
sections.content: The Transmission Date and Time data element contains the time the message is initiated by the terminal. Data and time format is MMDDhhmmss .

sections.heading: 3.6.6  SYSTEM TRACE AUDIT NUMBER  (P-11)
sections.content: The System Trace Audit Number is mandatory for both in the request and response messages. It carries a unique number in each transaction which is incremented by one for every transaction  from an acquiring system. A reversal and advice message for a corresponding authorisation has a different STAN. Reversal Repeats and Advice Repeats (0421/0121/0221) has the same STAN as that of an original reversal  and advice messages.

sections.heading: 3.6.7  LOCAL TRANSACTION TIME (P-12)
sections.content: The Local Transaction Time data element contains the local time at which the transaction began at the card acceptor location. Format for Local Transaction Time is ‘HHMMSS’.  This data element is mandatory in all incoming and outgoing messages apart from  Network Management messages.

sections.heading: HISO 8583 H2H MERCHANT  SPECIFICATION
sections.content: HISO 8583 Merchant Specification V3.9.9.docx  Page 33 of 119 Version : 3. 9.9

sections.heading: 3.6.8  LOCAL TRANSACTION DATE  (P-13)
sections.content: The Local Transaction Date  data  element contains the local month and day  when  the transaction began at the card acceptor location. Format for Local Transaction Date  is ‘MMDD ’. This data element is mandatory in all incoming and outgoing messages apart from  Network Management messages.

sections.heading: 3.6.9  EXPIRATION DATE  (P-14)
sections.content: The Expiration Date of the Card in the format YYMM. It is a conditional data element, not mandatory in all messages. A manual or keyed transaction requires expiration date data element for others it can be obtained from Track 2 data.  A value of 22/22 is acceptable for recurring transactions.

sections.heading: 3.6.10  MERCHANT TYPE  (P-18)
sections.content: The Merchant Type data element contains the Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) code of the retailer involved in the transaction. Do not populate without prior permission from Barclaycard Payments . This data element is mandatory in 0100 and 0200 messages for Payment Facilitator transactions, and remains optional for 0120/1, 0220/1 and 0420/1 messages.

sections.heading: 3.6.11  POINT OF SERVICE ENTRY MODE  (P-22)
sections.content: The Point of Service Entry Mode data element is a single field that contains two codes. The first code is two digits in length and indicates the method by which Track data or the primary account number (PAN) was entered into the system. The second code is one digit in length and indicates the entry capabilities available at the point of service. This data element is mandatory in 0100, 0200, 0120/1 and 0220/1  messages .  This data element is conditional in 0420/1. Pos 1 -2 Entry Mode  Pos. 3 Entry capability Valid values:

sections.content: Valid values:

sections.content: PIN (mPOS only)

sections.heading: HISO 8583 H2H MERCHANT  SPECIFICATION
sections.content: HISO 8583 Merchant Specification V3.9.9.docx  Page 34 of 119 Version : 3. 9.9

sections.heading: 3.6.12   CARD SEQUENCE  NUMBER  (P-23)
sections.content: The Card Sequence Number contains a number which distinguishes between different cards that have the same PAN. This number must be right justified and zero filled, or must contain three zeros. If the IC card does not have a Pan Sequence Number, then this field must not be populated

sections.heading: 3.6.13   POINT OF SERVICE CONDITION CODE  (P-25)
sections.content: The Point of Service Condition Code data element contains a code that identifies the condition under which the transaction is taking place at the point of service. Incoming POS  messages must contain this data element , BASE24  places the value in to the PT -SRV - COND -CDE field in the  PSTM. Supported  values:

sections.heading: 3.6.14   ACQUIRING INSTITUTION ID CODE  (P-32)
sections.content: The Acquiring Institution Identification Code data element contains a code that identifies the acquiring institution for the transaction, or its agent. The acquiring institution may be different from the card acceptor. This data element is  mandatory for all authorisation, financial and reversal messages, but  optional in Network Management messages.  Use default value of 492900

sections.heading: HISO 8583 H2H MERCHANT  SPECIFICATION
sections.content: HISO 8583 Merchant Specification V3.9.9.docx  Page 35 of 119 Version : 3. 9.9

sections.heading: 3.6.15   TRACK 2 DATA  (P-35)
sections.content: The Track 2 Data element is the information encoded on Track 2 of the magnetic stripe on the back of the card originating the transaction, excluding start and end sentinel and longitudinal redundancy check (LRC) characters. This data element includes the following; Primary account number (PAN), left justified (up to 19 digits) Field separator (=) Expiration date (YYMM) Service code (if present; 3 digits) Discretionary data (balance of available space)

sections.heading: 3.6.16   RETRIEVAL REFERENCE NUMBER  (P-37)
sections.content: A number assigned by the message initiator to uniquely identify a transaction. The number remains unchanged for all messages related to this transaction, i.e. a reversal advice should have the same number as the associated authorisation  transaction. Optional f ormat for P -37 - DDHHMM + STAN (6 digits) In case of reversal , advice  and completion advice  messages these will use the  Time and  STAN from the original data elements field. The values in original data elements have  the same values  as that of an original authorisation transaction.

sections.heading: 3.6.17   AUTHORISATION IDENTIFICATION RESPONSE (P-38)
sections.content: The Authorisation Identification Response data element contains a response identification number assigned by the authori sing institution  This field is required for 0120, 0220 and 0420 messages and must include the authorisation identification response data from the original authorisation message if required by scheme.

sections.heading: HISO 8583 H2H MERCHANT  SPECIFICATION
sections.content: HISO 8583 Merchant Specification V3.9.9.docx  Page 36 of 119 Version : 3. 9.9

sections.heading: 3.6.18   RESPONSE CODE (P-39)
sections.content: The Response Code data element contains a code that indicates the disposition of a message. This data element is mandatory for 0120, 0220, 0420 messages. For an 0120 or 0220 please refer to section 3.7.14  in this guide. For an 0420 valid values are :

sections.heading: 3.6.19  CARD ACCEPTOR TERMINAL IDENTIFICATION (P-41)
sections.content: The Card Acceptor Terminal Identification data element contains a unique code identifying the terminal at the card acceptor location . This data element is mandatory in all messages excluding Network Management messages. This field must be left justified and filled.

sections.heading: 3.6.20   CARD ACCEPTOR IDENTIFICATION CODE (P-42)
sections.content: The Card Acceptor Identification Code contains the Merchant Id of a card acceptor. This value is defined by the acquirer. This data element is  optional . This field must be left justified and filled.

sections.heading: 3.6.21   CARD ACCEPTOR NAME/LOCATION (P-43)
sections.content: The Card Acceptor Name/Location data element contains the name and location of the card acceptor that defines the point of service in both local and interchange environments . The Card Acceptor Name/Location data element is mandatory in all 0100, 0120, 0121, 0200, 0220, 0221, 0420, and 0421 messages. The structure of this field is  defined  as below;

sections.heading: HISO 8583 H2H MERCHANT  SPECIFICATION
sections.content: HISO 8583 Merchant Specification V3.9.9.docx  Page 37 of 119 Version : 3. 9.9

sections.heading: Position  Length  Description
sections.content: 1-22 22 Terminal Owner The name of the institution owning the terminal 23-35 13 Terminal City The city in which the transaction -originating terminal is located 36-38 3 Terminal State A code indicating the state or province in which the transaction -originating terminal is located . is a valid value in this field 39-40 2 Terminal Country A code indicating the country in which the transaction originating terminal is located  (i.e GB, DE, IT..etc...)

sections.heading: 3.6.22   RETAILER DATA (P-48)
sections.content: The Retailer Data element carries the information required to identify the retailer involved in the transaction. sections.heading: 3.6.23   CURRENCY CODE (P-49)
sections.content: The code in the Transaction Currency Code data element identifies the currency that applies to the Transaction Amount (P -4) data element. It is mandatory for all authorisation , financial transaction, and reversal messages. Currency code used must be according to the ISO standard.

sections.heading: 3.6.24   PIN DATA (P-52)
sections.content: This data element contains the encrypted PIN block . This data element is only used for Online PIN verification transaction. Also this data element is a conditional field in 0100 and 0200 messages. Must not be present for OCTs. PIN data field is not used in the response messages. Standard Triple DES is supported. For more details refer to APPENDIX A

sections.heading: 3.6.25   ADDITIONAL AMOUNTS (P-54)
sections.content: This data element contains the Additional Amounts which  carries the cash back amount for purchase  with cashback or cashback without purchase transactions  where cash is being returned to the customer. This data element is only used for cashback  transaction s. If the transaction is a p urchase  with cashback or cashback without purchase , the  Additional Amounts data element must be included field in 0100 /0200 and 0 420 messages.  Otherwise the field should not be present for non cashback transaction. The structure of this field is defined as below;

sections.heading: 3.7.1 SECONDARY BITMAP (P-1)
sections.content: If any of the secondary bitmap data element (64 -128) present in the response message, then this field is required.

sections.heading: 3.7.2  PRIMARY ACCOUNT NUMBER (P-2)
sections.content: The Primary Account Number data element contains the primary account number (PAN) of the cardholder account involved in the transaction . It is optional in the response messages. This data element is mandatory in reversal  response (0430)  message s.

sections.heading: 3.7.3  PROCESSING CODE (P-3)
sections.content: The Processing Code data element is a mandatory field in the response message;  the value in this field is echoed from the request message.

sections.heading: 3.7.4  TRANSACTION AMOUNT (P-4)
sections.content: The Transaction Amount data element  is a mandatory field in the response message This data field may sometimes carry zero amount in 0 130, 0230 messages. This data field may contain an amount which differs (typically lower) to the original authorised amount for an 0110/0210 for partially approved transactions.

sections.heading: 3.7.5  TRANSMISSION DATE AND TIME (P-7)
sections.content: The Transmission Date and Time data element contains the  system  time the message is delivered by the host . Data and time format is MMDDhhmmss.

sections.heading: 3.7.6  SYSTEM TRACE AUDIT NUMBER (P-11)
sections.content: The System Trace Audit Number is mandatory for both in the request and response messages. The value in this field is echoed from the request message.

sections.heading: HISO 8583 H2H MERCHANT  SPECIFICATION
sections.content: HISO 8583 Merchant Specification V3.9.9.docx  Page 43 of 119 Version : 3. 9.9

sections.heading: 3.7.7  LOCAL TRANSACTION TIME (P-12)
sections.content: The Local Transaction Time data element contains the local time at which the transaction began at the card acceptor location. Format for Local Transaction Time is ‘HHMMSS’.  This data element is mandatory for  all response  messages apart from  Network Management messages.  The value in this field is echoed from the request message.

sections.heading: 3.7.8  LOCAL TRANSACTION DATE (P-13)
sections.content: The Local Transaction Date data element contains the local month and day when the transaction began at the card acceptor location. Format for Local Transaction Date is ‘MMDD’. This data element is mandatory for all response messages apart from  Network Management messages. The value in this field is echoed from the request message.

sections.heading: 3.7.9  DATE, SETTLEMENT  (P-15)
sections.content: Date, Settlement (DE 15) is the logical settlement date  based on the Acquirer date. This data element is present in all F inancial Transaction  respones (0210/0230 ) and Reversal response (0430) messages that convey a settlement value. It  contains the calendar date on which funds for a transaction will be  settled.  Format for Date , Settlement  is ‘DD MM’. Will only be returned if the draft capture flag is set to ‘1’ in field S -125 position 14.

sections.heading: 3.7.10   POINT OF SERVICE CONDITION CODE (P-25)
sections.content: The Point of Service Condition Code is conditional  in the response messages. The value in this field is echoed from the request message.

sections.heading: 3.7.11   ACQUIRING INSTITUTION ID CODE (P-32)
sections.content: This data element is mandatory for all response messages (0110, 0130, 0210, 0230 and  0430 ). The value in this field is echoed from the request message.

sections.heading: 3.7.12   RETRIEVAL REFERENCE NUMBER (P-37)
sections.content: If present, echo the value from the corresponding request message.

sections.heading: 3.7.13   AUTHORISATION IDENTIFICATION RESPONSE (P-38)
sections.content: The Authorisation  Identification Response data element contains a response identification number assigned by the authori sing institution. The Authorisation  Identification Response data element is mandatory for 0420/1 messages .

sections.heading: 6.1     3DES  ENCRYPTION
sections.content: Triple Data Encryption Standard, The term “Triple DES” is used to denote DES cryptography performed with double length keys. This is sometimes known as EDE mode (Encrypt -Decrypt -

sections.heading: Encrypt)
sections.content: The Triple DES PIN block encryption method supported using the TDEA Electronic Code Book (TECB) as described in ANSI X9.52 standard. The supported PIN block  format is ISO 9564 format ‘0’ is also known as ANSI X9.8 PIN block format . PIN Translation: PIN Translation refers to the “decryption and re-encryption” of PIN data from one security zone to another. Usually this is necessitated when there are more than one security zones between the PIN originating and verification location. Decrypt the PIN using the secret PIN encryption key that exists between the merchant and Barclay’s. Re-encrypt the PIN under another secret key between Barclay’s and the Card Scheme before forwarding to the Card Schemes.
